STI Breaks Ground on 2.6 Trillion Won Power Semiconductor Materials Base in China

# STI Breaks Ground on 2.6 Trillion Won Power Semiconductor Materials Base in China

## First-phase AMB ceramic substrate plant begins construction in Guangzhou, with pilot production targeted for September 2027

* KIMGEON
* Published 2026.07.02 19:23
* Reply 0

Character Size Settings

가

가

The body content of the article will be changed to this letter size.

이 기사를 공유합니다

STI has begun construction of its power semiconductor materials production base in Guangzhou, China, about four months after signing an investment agreement with the city's Baiyun District government.

According to Baiyun District in Guangzhou on July 2, Donghan Semiconductor Guangdong Co., Ltd., STI's Chinese subsidiary, held a groundbreaking ceremony on June 25 for its Guangzhou manufacturing base in the Future Industry Innovation Core Zone of the Guangzhou Private Science and Technology Park in Baiyun District.

STI signed an investment agreement on Feb. 5 with the Baiyun District People's Government to build a smart manufacturing base for power semiconductors. The total investment announced at the time was 12.4 billion yuan (about 2.6 trillion won). Donghan Semiconductor Guangdong Co., Ltd. was established on Feb. 9.

The first phase of the Guangzhou project will involve an investment of 2.3 billion yuan (approximately 527 billion won). The facility will occupy a 50,000-square-meter site and manufacture active metal brazing (AMB) ceramic substrates for power semiconductor modules. Construction is scheduled for completion with pilot production beginning in September 2027. Once fully operational, the plant is expected to generate annual output worth more than 3 billion yuan (approximately 687.4 billion won).

AMB ceramic substrates are a key base material for power semiconductor modules. They are used in electric vehicles, smart power grids, 5G communications and aerospace applications.

Baiyun District described the project as the first foreign-funded manufacturing investment in the district this year exceeding 10 billion yuan. The district said the project would strengthen the power semiconductor supply chain in the Guangdong-Hong Kong-Macao Greater Bay Area while attracting high-end technical talent and related industrial resources.

Baiyun District also plans to link the project with the South China research and development center of semiconductor design company VeriSilicon. It said the initiative would help establish connections between chip design and advanced materials manufacturing while accelerating the development of a semiconductor ecosystem spanning design, manufacturing, packaging and testing.
